content:
🚀 netsnap | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Quickly and easily switching between different network configurations on a Windows machine, such as moving between office, home, and on-site network environments, without manual GUI interaction.
✨ Key Features: • CLI utility for network profile switching on Windows • Store network configurations as named profiles in JSON • Apply profiles with a single command (e.g., `netsnap apply work`) • Supports static IP, DHCP, gateway, and DNS settings
📖 Summary: netsnap is a command-line utility for Windows that simplifies switching between pre-configured network profiles. It allows users to save and apply different network settings (IP address, gateway, DNS) with a single command, eliminating the need for manual configuration through the GUI.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
